title: 理論に基づいた昔ばなし

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
One day, I spot Biwa Hayahide in the library,
looking perplexed about something. I stroll over
to say hello and ask what's wrong.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Lately, I've been reading picture books to
children as an extracurricular activity, but... it's
just not clicking with me.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Well... You know how fairy tales and legends
aren't exactly true to life?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Whenever I read such stories, I can't help but get
distracted by all the errors in logic and
inconsistencies in them.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I think I can see where she's coming from, but I
don't fully understand what she means.
choice_data_list:
Can you read one of those stories to me and
point out what you mean?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: Sure, that seems like the best approach. Allow me to read Momotaro for you.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: "Once upon a time, in a land far away..."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"An old woman found a giant peach and took it
home."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"She and her husband go to cut the peach, when
suddenly..."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
See, here's the part where my suspension of
disbelief starts to break down.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
I already had trouble swallowing the fact that a
giant peach was floating down the river out of
nowhere...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
But then we discover that Momotaro was inside of
the peach the whole time.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Based on the information available, we have to
assume that he must have been inside it for
anywhere between a few minutes to an hour.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
If that's the case, how did he not die of
asphyxiation?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Then there's the part where the old man and
woman cut the peach in half.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
How did they avoid cutting Momotaro in half
too? Did they take care to cut around him or
something?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
But that wouldn't make any sense since they
didn't even know he was in there.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Even if I am, what benefit is there to learn about
things that are logically impossible?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
I'd rather not take part in teaching falsities to
children.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
From the look on her face, I can tell she's truly
dedicated to this. If she's going to take things this
seriously, then...
choice_data_list: Why don't you put your own twist on things?
Why don't you put your own twist on things?
Why don't you make it a study group activity?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
From the look on her face, I can tell she's truly
dedicated to this. If she's going to take things this
seriously, then...
choice_data_list: Why don't you put your own twist on things?
Why don't you put your own twist on things?
Why don't you make it a study group activity?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: My own twist?
choice_data_list: Yeah. Try inserting some logic in the stories.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
You mean reconstruct them in a way that makes
sense? That does sound intriguing...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
When it's time for Biwa Hayahide to read to the
children again, she put her new tactic to work.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"An old woman found a giant peach and took it
home."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"The old man and woman were puzzled by the
strange hole in the peach and realized it must've
been a breathing hole."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"Together, the two of them cut into the peach
very, very carefully."

text_block_list.name: Energetic Child
text_block_list.text:
The peach had a breathing hole in it? I don't
remember that part...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
But if there wasn't one, Momotaro wouldn't be
able to breathe inside the peach.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
It would be very sad if that were the case, don't
you think?

text_block_list.name: Precocious Child
text_block_list.text: Yeah! It would be super, duper sad!

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Hehe. A kind stranger must have thought the
same and made that hole for him.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: Now, back to the story...

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"Momotaro emerged from the peach hale and
whole thanks to the breathing hole."

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
"And because the old man and woman had cut
into the peach so carefully, he didn't have a
single scratch on him."

text_block_list.name: Energetic Child
text_block_list.text: Yay! He was okay!

text_block_list.name: Precocious Child
text_block_list.text: Ehehe! Good for Momotaro!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Biwa Hayahide's logically rewritten children's
stories initially cause some confusion...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
But in the end, it was a highly educational
experience for the children.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text: A study group, you say?

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
The children might find such a thing dull, but
holding a group discussion might be a good way
to discuss the flaws in such stories.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Yes, I like that idea! I'll start preparing for our
discussion right away!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
When it's time for Biwa Hayahide to read to the
children again, she carefully presents her ideas to
her audience.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
The glass slippers Cinderella wore may look
beautiful, but they're actually incredibly
dangerous.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Because glass is hard, the likelihood of injuring
your feet while wearing them is high.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
And if they were to break, they would leave glass
shards everywhere.

text_block_list.name: Energetic Child
text_block_list.text: Wow! That would be really bad!

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Exactly. That's why you should wear shoes made
from safe materials and not glass slippers, even if
they're from a fairy godmother.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
When the Big Bad Wolf asked Little Red Riding
Hood where she was going, she told him, thus
revealing the location of her grandmother's
house.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
However, if she had kept her guard up and
refused to answer, her grandmother would've
escaped the tragic fate of being eaten.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
The moral of the story is that you should run away
from a stranger if they try to talk to you.
Understood?

text_block_list.name: Precocious Child
text_block_list.text: Yeah!

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Although picture books can be quite charming,
they occasionally contain problematic elements.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
You have to learn how to identify these problems
and avoid them in order to live a good life.

text_block_list.name: Biwa Hayahide
text_block_list.text:
Now, were you all able to learn something new
from today's story time?

text_block_list.name: Children
text_block_list.text: Yeah! We did!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Biwa Hayahide's educational story time ends up
being a big hit.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Not only did the children love her, but she seems
to have greatly enjoyed herself too.
